setupGuide.notes: Placeholder for Melbourne Grand Prix Circuit — 58 laps / 307.6 km reproduced as 96 laps of Adelaide Modern. Modern Adelaide keeps the Australian parkland-circuit character.
No 1997 Interlagos layout in AMS2; the venue is real, so the historical lap count is kept on the era-close 1991 layout.

The 1997 Argentine GP ran Circuito No. 6 at the Gálvez autodrome; AMS2's Buenos Aires Circuito 6T is the 1990s Grand Prix configuration of the same venue (4.259 km — an exact length match), so the historical lap count is kept.

track.realVenue: Autodromo Internazionale Enzo e Dino Ferrari

setupGuide.notes: No 1997 Imola in AMS2; the venue is real, so the historical lap count is kept on the era-close 2001 layout.

1997 Monaco predates the 2003-04 harbour-front reprofiles, so the 1988 layout is the era-closest; the venue is real and the historical lap count is kept (the 1997 race ran 62 laps under the two-hour rule in the wet — f1db's official race length).

1997 Montreal is the post-1996 configuration of the same venue as Montreal Modern, so the historical lap count is kept; the race was red-flagged on lap 54 of 69 and f1db's official 54-lap race length is used.

Placeholder for Circuit de Nevers Magny-Cours — 72 laps / 306 km reproduced as 73 laps of Le Mans Circuit Bugatti. Keeps the French GP at a French Grand Prix venue (the curated 1991+ era override avoids double-booking this season's Spanish GP at Barcelona).
No 1997 Silverstone in AMS2; the venue is real, so the historical lap count is kept on the near-identical 2001 layout.
No 1997 Hockenheim in AMS2; the venue is real, so the historical lap count is kept on the near-identical 2001 layout of the pre-2002 forest circuit.
AMS2 ships the 2025 Hungaroring; the venue is real and largely unchanged in character since 1986, so the historical lap count is kept.
No 1997 Spa layout in AMS2; the venue is real, so the historical lap count is kept on the era-closest 1993 layout.
No 1997 Monza in AMS2; the venue is real, so the historical lap count is kept on the era-matching 1991 layout (pre-2000 first-chicane configuration).
The 1997 Austrian GP ran the A1-Ring; AMS2's Spielberg 2001 is that same layout (f1db lists the venue under its current Red Bull Ring name), so the historical lap count is kept.
The 1997 Luxembourg GP ran the Nürburgring GP-Strecke before the 2002 Mercedes-Arena extension; the venue is real, so the historical lap count is kept on the modern GP layout.
Suzuka appears in AMS2 as Kansai; the venue is real, so the historical lap count is kept on the modern GP layout.
1997 Jerez is the post-1994 configuration with the Senna chicane; Jerez 2019 is the same venue at the matching 4.43 km lap length, so the historical lap count is kept.
